methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Space42, a publicly listed entity on the ADX formed through the merger of Bayanat and Yahsat, operates in the Space & Aerospace sector. The company has raised $695M+ in ECA financing. The comparable universe comprises 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private firms including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, though the private peer set skews toward substantially larger, later-stage entities.

Two standard valuation methodologies prove inapplicable to this target. Peer-median analysis fails due to insufficient narrow comparables: of 23 same-sector peers identified, none satisfy simultaneous filters for stage, adjacency, and valuation tier—a minimum of 3 datapoints is required for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring cannot proceed because Space42's revenue is not on file; while the Space & Aerospace public sector median trades at 64.2x P/S across the 6 available tickers, this multiple cannot be applied without the target's top-line figure.

The absence of applicable quantitative triangulation methods limits precision. A credible valuation would require either: disclosure of Space42's revenue (enabling P/S anchor), identification of same-stage private transactions at comparable scale, or forward guidance permitting DCF calibration. Until such data surfaces, valuation ranges remain unsupported by the available analytics. The key uncertainty is revenue scale relative to the public peer set.
